Off Calendar Nov-29-2023 Set For Trial On Sep-23-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Re-Notice Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT POPPI METAXAS, GERASIMOS METAXAS, IONIAN VENTURE PARTNERS, LLC AND 1415 PACHECO, LLC's Re-Notice Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint is overruled.

The 2014 Undertaking provides that defendant must repay the defense costs when it is "finally established that the Insurer has no liability for such Defense Costs under the terms, conditions, exclusions, or limitations of the Policy." (emphasis added.)

The 2014 Undertaking states that "finally established" means that the defendant has pleaded guilty or her conviction is final. The parties agree to the following timeline of events: (1) Defendant pleaded guilty on 4/30/15; (2) plaintiff demanded repayment and commenced CGC-16-554004 on 9/1/16; (3) on 5/4/17, defendant filed a motion to vacate judgment to vacate her plea; (4) the plea became final on 11/17/20 when the appellate court issued the mandate; (5) plaintiff filed the original complaint in this case on 8/17/21 and the first amended complaint on 2/9/24.

Defendants fail to show that the second cause of action is time barred. First, the doctrine of equitable tolling applied (at the very least) while defendant attempted to vacate her plea. In McDonald v. Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 99-100, the court explains:

The equitable tolling of statutes of limitations is a judicially created, nonstatutory doctrine. It is designed to prevent unjust and technical forfeitures of the right to a trial on the merits when the purpose of the statute of limitations-timely notice to the defendant of the plaintiff's claims-has been satisfied. Where applicable, the doctrine will suspend or extend a statute of limitations as necessary to ensure fundamental practicality and fairness. Its application in such circumstances serves the need for harmony and the avoidance of chaos in the administration of justice. Tolling eases the pressure on parties concurrently to seek redress in two separate forums with the attendant danger of conflicting decisions on the same issue. (citations and internal quotations omitted).

Here, it would make no sense for plaintiff to continue to pursue case CGC-16-554004 while defendant sought to unwind the plea, the event that triggered plaintiff's right to sue in that case.

Second, the 2014 Undertaking is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation that since the plea was under attack, plaintiff could rely on the second triggering event which had yet to become final. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) The term "finally established" contemplated that defendant's criminal proceedings would be over. But since she pursued appellate relief, the resolution of the "finally established" issue was delayed.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca. Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JAMES DAVID CURRAN, Esq. of Wolkin Curran, LLP (Tel: 415-982-9390) for Plaintiff.
Atty. CRAIG DANIEL, Esq. of Gluck Daniel Atkinson LLP (Tel: 415-510-2114) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ST. PAUL MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to amend complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-8; see also CCP 428.50.) Here, the trial date is not until September 23. A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
